headline: Australia: AEMO’s 2026 ISP calls for nearly 120GW of utility-scale wind and solar by 2050
summary_80w: Australia needs to build nearly 120GW of utility-scale wind and solar by 2050, approximately five times the current level, says AEMO.

headline: EU tripartite agreement targets 30-35GW of storage deployments by 2028
summary_80w: The European Union (EU) has announced a tripartite agreement to accelerate energy storage deployments in the next two years.

headline: Envision to supply 660MWh of battery storage for South Africa’s ‘largest private hybrid power project’
summary_80w: Envision has signed a 660MWh BESS supply agreement with SOLA Group and construction company WBHO in South Africa's Free State.

headline: AGL submits 50MW/100MWh Awaba BESS to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Australian utility AGL Energy has submitted a 100MWh BESS in New South Wales for federal environmental assessment under the EPBC Act.

headline: ‘Decisions look obvious after the event. They rarely are in real time’: OptiGrid on South Australia’s battery storage price cap event
summary_80w: OptiGrid's Sahand Karimi examines why state of charge & algorithm quality drove divergent outcomes during SA1's AU$20,300/MWh price cap on 21 June.

headline: Waaree expands EPC footprint through US$129 million acquisition
summary_80w: Waaree Renewable Technologies has acquired a 55% stake in Associated Power Structures for INR12.25 billion (US$129 million).

headline: Intersolar 2026: Key takeaways from a week with Europe’s solar industry
summary_80w: Europe’s solar industry seemed a little downbeat as it trudged to Munich for Intersolar Europe 2026 this week. Under the unforgiving June heat, PV Tech took the temperature of the industry.

headline: VinEnergo, SunAsia to develop 422MWp floating solar portfolio in Philippines
summary_80w: VinEnergo and SunAsia Energy have partnered to develop 422MWp of floating solar projects in the Philippines.

headline: Inverters could be Nextpower’s next steps after Prevalon, Zimmermann deals
summary_80w: PV Tech spoke with Bloomberg Intelligence about Nextpower's acquisition of Zimmermann and how this was a logical next step.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Astronergy showcases ASTRO N7s 3.0 residential PV module
summary_80w: Chinese solar manufacturer Astronergy has launched ASTRO N7s 3.0, its latest residential solar module, at Intersolar Europe 2026.

headline: Enlight secures financing for 1.2GW/4GWh Arizona solar and storage ‘Complex’
summary_80w: IPP Enlight Renewable Energy has announced that its US subsidiary Clēnera Holdings has entered into a debt financing framework agreement for the CO Bar Complex in Arizona, US.

headline: UK’s Ofgem goes big on LDES cap-and-floor window 1; 18-hour lithium-ion among winners
summary_80w: UK energy regulator Ofgem has shortlisted 16 projects for the first ever long-duration energy storage (LDES) cap-and-floor scheme, totalling 7.6GW of capacity ranging from 8- to 22-hour durations.

headline: Energy Dome’s second CO2 Battery project with Google will secure carbon-free energy for tech giant while easing grid congestion in Ireland
summary_80w: Energy Dome has signed its second low-carbon energy supply contract with Google.

headline: Asian Development Bank supports 500MWh grid-forming battery energy storage project in Cambodia
summary_80w: ADB has approved a Royal Government of Cambodia application for financial support for a 250MW/500MWh battery storage project.

headline: Goldwind Capital submits 1GWh wind-plus-storage site to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Goldwind Capital has submitted the Milpulling Wind Farm, a 1GWh wind-plus-storage site for assessment under Australia's EPBC Act.

headline: Trends to look out for at Energy Storage Summit Asia 2026
summary_80w: Energy Storage Summit Asia 2026 convenes in Bangkok as Vietnam's tariff reform, Cambodia's grid milestone, and AI demand reshape the region.

headline: Pathfinder secures €100 million to finance 400MW solar, 200MW storage portfolio in the UK
summary_80w: Pathfinder Clean Energy (PACE) has secured a €100 million investment to support its deployment of a solar and BESS portfolio in the UK.

headline: Galileo advances 630MW French renewable energy pipeline
summary_80w: Galileo is advancing a 630MW renewables pipeline in France, with permitting applications for 11 projects due in the coming months.

headline: R.Power secures finance for 75MWp Romanian PV portfolio
summary_80w: R.Power has secured a €41.6 million (US$47.3 million) project finance facility for four solar projects in Romania with a combined capacity of approximately 75MWp.

headline: NLC India, OREDA sign JV to develop 1GW of renewable energy in Odisha
summary_80w: NLC India and OREDA have signed a joint venture agreement to develop 1,000MW of renewable energy capacity in Odisha.

headline: ITRPV 2026: Solar industry maintains historic learning curve despite market turbulence
summary_80w: The annual ITRPV report was published this week, offering a snapshot of the latest technological trends shaping the industry.

headline: US ROUND-UP: Vesper, rPlus and Matrix advance PV projects across the country
summary_80w: US developers Vesper Energy, rPlus Energies and Matrix Renewables have announced a number of project advancements in the US this week.

headline: Tesla, Sunrun, Renew Home announce 16GW VPP framework, focus on PJM
summary_80w: US residential solar and energy storage installer Sunrun, energy management platform Renew Home, and Tesla have announced an agreement to deliver more than 16GW of flexible energy capacity to US hyperscalers and utilities.

headline: US ROUNDUP: e-Storage, Spearmint Energy, and OCI Energy advance BESS projects in Michigan and Texas
summary_80w: In this US news roundup, e-Storage, Spearmint Energy, and OCI Energy advance battery energy storage system (BESS) projects and financing in Michigan and Texas.

headline: Sodium-ion will reach cost parity with LFP ‘in two to three years’, BESS firm says
summary_80w: Sodium-ion BESS startup Moonwatt expects the battery chemistry to reach price cost parity with lithium iron phosphate (LFP) in the next two or three years, according to its CCO, Valentin Rota.

headline: Nextpower and Prevalon CEOs discuss acquisition background and future strategy
summary_80w: ESN Premium speaks with Nextpower CEO Dan Shugar and Prevalon’s Tom Cornell about the solar PV solutions company’s (re)entry into the energy storage space.

headline: ees Europe 2026: Grid-forming PCS, DC-coupling, C&I and data centre solutions among product launches and market debuts
summary_80w: With ees Europe taking place in Munich, Germany, this week alongside Intersolar Europe and Smarter E, Energy-Storage.news takes a look at some expo highlights.

headline: Australia: AEMO calls for 35GW of short and medium-duration storage plus 5GW of long-duration storage by 2050
summary_80w: AEMO has set out a storage requirement of almost 50GW in its latest 2026 Integrated System Plan (ISP), split between 35GW of short and medium-duration battery storage for daily firming and 5GW of long-duration energy storage (LDES) for seasonal reliability.

headline: Germany’s battery energy storage market is entering a new phase of maturity
summary_80w: Germany's policymakers, regulators and energy storage industry must carefully consider the issues of grid connections and grid fees, writes Thomas Antonioli of developer Terra One.

headline: Toyo Solar sells shares to finance HJT cell manufacturing in the US
summary_80w: Toyo Solar has raised around US$50 million to finance the expansion of the company’s cell manufacturing work in Houston, Texas.

headline: Comstock Metals to establish solar panel recycling and logistics hub in Ohio

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: Domestic solar manufacturing won’t be a ‘disruptor’, Fronius pushes EU-made message
summary_80w: Europe will not be able to produce a “disruption” in the solar manufacturing industry, according to Peter Fath, CEO of RCT Solutions.

headline: Intersolar Europe 2026: GCL-SI launches GPC 3.0 all-black back-contact module
summary_80w: GCL System Integration Technology (GCL-SI) has set back-contact (BC) cell technology as the core pillar of its next-generation PV roadmap.

summary_80w: Sungrow has launched its SG125CX-P3 string inverter, designed for use in the C&I sector, at the ongoing Intersolar Europe trade show.

headline: China issues renewables consumption rules set to fuel new growth cycle for solar PV and energy storage

summary_80w: LONGi has launched its Hi-MO 9 Prime series of modules at Intersolar Europe 2026, which have a conversion efficiency of 25.2%.

headline: How LONGi is driving true technological value

headline: Dominion Energy launches long-duration energy storage RFI in Virginia

summary_80w: ESN Premium heard from executives at battery energy storage system (BESS) firms Rept and Fluence on the EU funding ban on Chinese inverters in solar and BESS projects.

headline: US energy storage installations will quadruple by 2031, Wood Mackenzie forecasts

headline: Grid-forming technology explored and explained: Download our white paper, with Kehua Digital Energy & TÜV Rheinland

headline: ZincFive bets on revenue track record in US$752 million SPAC merger after 2021 cohort struggles

summary_80w: CATL held the global launch of its large-scale sodium-ion BESS yesterday, unveiling a 30MWh+ module which it will ship outside China from next year.

headline: Defying the PV industry downturn: how PV-storage companies survive and thrive globally
summary_80w: PV Tech spoke with Hanersun's chairman about the company's PV-storage strategy, global expansion and the Chinese market outlook.

headline: Reigniting Europe’s solar market

headline: World adds 664GW of solar PV in 2025, exceeds 3TW of cumulative capacity
summary_80w: The world added a record 664GW of new solar PV capacity in 2025, pushing cumulative global operational solar capacity above 3TW.

summary_80w: Trina Solar has secured an order from a global distributed energy customer for its perovskite/crystalline silicon tandem solar modules.

headline: There is now more installed energy storage capacity than nuclear in Europe, yet ‘no country has reached its potential’

headline: SEIA map shows solar uses 0.07% of US farmland

summary_80w: Module quality and reliability concerns, HJT, manufacturing in Africa and hail were among the key topics discussed at PV ModuleTech USA.

headline: Sonnedix secures Spanish electricity supply licence

headline: Before you worry about voltage drops – worry about data drops

headline: Scatec reaches financial close on 120MW Tunisian solar project

headline: Enphase co-founder says solid-state transformer can ease data centre ‘power problems’

headline: US sodium-ion startups form coalition to challenge China’s battery dominance
summary_80w: A group of sodium-ion (Na-ion) battery-focused companies have launched a new industry coalition, the American Battery Leadership Coalition (ABLC).

headline: Aypa Power brings 1,000MWh Arizona BESS online in utility Salt River Project’s busy June
summary_80w: Energy storage developer-operator Aypa Power and utility Salt River Project (SRP) have brought the 250MW/1,000MWh Pediment energy storage project online in Mesa, Arizona.

summary_80w: We chat to Stephan Lehrke, head of Germany at Second Foundation, a Czech Republic-headquartered energy asset trading firm which is becoming an owner-operator of BESS.

headline: CEE Roundup: EPCs complete CATL and Sungrow-supplied 600MWh Bulgaria projects

headline: Neoen Australia signs virtual battery deals for 2.3GWh Western Downs Battery

headline: Limited synergies and profitability drive Wärtsilä’s energy storage divestment

headline: EU Roundup: Octopus and Eco Stor financing deals for large-scale BESS in Italy and Germany enable 1.6GWh of capacity

Optimiser Entrix has also won two large contracts in Germany and the Netherlands.

headline: Solar Energy Corporation of India invites bids for 4.8GWh of energy storage-backed FDRE capacity
